chunks.chunk_preview: IN GRAM O MICRO CLOUD  Solution Design Document  ABC Ltd. Group Limited Database Re-host Design  Aug 22, 2019 Version 1.9  Ingram Micro Cloud Corporate headquar

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO not declared
findings.Description: The section does not declare Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO) for the production workload, which must be defined during design review and used to determine backup frequency, redundancy, and DR patterns.

findings.Issue: Backup and disaster recovery strategy not defined
findings.Description: No description of backup mechanisms (e.g., Azure Backup, PaaS native backups) or disaster recovery patterns aligned to workload tier is provided in this section.

findings.Issue: DR testing requirement not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not state that the workload’s disaster recovery plan will be tested at least annually through a tabletop or live failover exercise.

findings.Issue: Mandatory tagging strategy not defined
findings.Description: The section does not define the required Azure resource tag set (costCenter, owner, environment, workload, dataClassification, slaTier) or enforcement via Azure Policy.

findings.Issue: Budget and cost monitoring controls not described
findings.Description: No mention of subscription or workload budgets, alert thresholds (50/80/100 percent), or cost anomaly detection as required for cost governance.

findings.Issue: Azure resource naming convention not defined
findings.Description: Although a table of contents references Azure naming conventions, this section does not define the required deterministic naming pattern (<resourceType>-<workload>-<environment>-<region>-<instance>) or hash suffix rules for global resources.

findings.Issue: Resource organization and lifecycle boundaries not described
findings.Description: The section does not describe the policy requirement that resource groups align with lifecycle boundaries and must not mix permanent and ephemeral resources.

findings.Issue: Infrastructure as Code requirement not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not specify that Azure infrastructure must be defined as code (Bicep preferred, Terraform acceptable) with source controlled modules and CI/CD enforcement.

findings.Issue: CI/CD security and identity practices not described
findings.Description: No mention of pipeline requirements such as managed agents, workload identity federation, prohibition of long‑lived service principals or PATs, or retrieving deployment secrets from Key Vault.

findings.Issue: Azure Policy governance model not described
findings.Description: The section does not describe governance through Azure Policy assignments at the management group root, regulatory initiatives, or policy enforcement strategies across environments.

## Document Review  After creating an initial draft by Ingram Micro Cloud Architect, the design document will be peer reviewed internally. The process is descri

findings.Issue: Source control and branch protection not specified
findings.Description: The section describes manual peer review of the document but does not state that the source of truth is maintained in Git with branch protection, required reviewers, signed commits, and status checks as required by policy.

findings.Issue: Infrastructure-as-Code governance not referenced
findings.Description: The review process does not mention that Azure infrastructure must be defined as code (Bicep by default, Terraform acceptable, ARM JSON deprecated) or that design artifacts should align with this requirement.

findings.Issue: CI/CD pipeline governance controls not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not reference the required CI/CD controls such as managed pipeline agents, workload-identity federation authentication to Azure, security scanning, linting, unit tests, and plan/what-if validation during pull requests.

findings.Issue: Environment promotion and approval gates not described
findings.Description: Policies require deployments to be promoted through environments (dev, test, stage, prod) with approval gates and automated smoke tests. The document review section does not reference this release governance model.

chunks.chunk_preview: ### 1.1 Preface  ABC Ltd. Co-operative Group Limited is a New Zealand multinational dairy co-operative owned by around 10,500 New Zealand farmers. It is backed

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO not defined
findings.Description: The section introduces the migration and design effort but does not declare Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO) for the production workloads. The policy requires that every production workload defines RPO and RTO during design review to guide redundancy, backup frequency, and disaster recovery patterns.

findings.Issue: Observability and telemetry approach not described
findings.Description: The section does not mention how telemetry, application logs, HTTP logs, platform metrics, or diagnostics will be emitted to a central Log Analytics workspace with Application Insights. The policy requires centralized observability and diagnostic settings for all resources.

findings.Issue: Secrets management strategy not defined
findings.Description: The section does not describe how secrets such as connection strings, API keys, or certificates will be managed. Policy requires secrets to be stored exclusively in Azure Key Vault and referenced by applications rather than stored in configuration or application settings.

findings.Issue: Data protection and encryption approach not defined
findings.Description: The section does not describe data classification, encryption at rest using customer-managed keys for confidential or restricted data, or TLS 1.2+ enforcement for data in transit. Policies require encryption and data protection controls to be defined based on data sensitivity tiers.

chunks.chunk_preview: ### 1.2 Audience  · ABC Ltd. Services technical team need to provide feedback of all the migration relevant documents mentioned in section 1.1.  · Ingram Micro
### 1.3 Customer requirement  ABC Ltd. would like to move on-premise workload to Azure to solve the following challenges:  · Increase the quality, transparency

findings.Issue: Identity and access management controls not specified
findings.Description: The section does not describe how identities will be issued from a single Microsoft Entra ID tenant, use of Privileged Identity Management, Conditional Access with phishing‑resistant MFA, or the use of managed identities for workloads as required by the policy.

findings.Issue: Network security architecture not defined
findings.Description: The requirements do not mention the mandated hub-and-spoke network topology, centralized inspection via Azure Firewall, or segmentation with NSGs and Application Security Groups. Policies require these controls for Azure network design.

findings.Issue: Private endpoint usage for PaaS services not addressed
findings.Description: Policies require Private Endpoints with public network access disabled for PaaS services such as Storage, Key Vault, SQL, Cosmos DB, Service Bus, and Event Hubs. The section does not state that these controls will be implemented.

findings.Issue: Data classification framework not defined
findings.Description: The policy requires each workload to maintain a data inventory mapped to sensitivity tiers (public, internal, confidential, restricted) that drive encryption and retention controls. The section mentions document classification but does not define the required tiered data classification model.

findings.Issue: Encryption and key management approach not specified
findings.Description: There is no mention of mandatory encryption at rest, TLS 1.2+ for encryption in transit, or the use of customer-managed keys stored in Azure Key Vault Premium for confidential or restricted data as required by policy.

findings.Issue: Secrets management via Key Vault not addressed
findings.Description: Policies require all secrets (connection strings, API keys, certificates) to be stored in Azure Key Vault and referenced from configuration rather than embedded. The section does not describe any secret management approach.

findings.Issue: Backup approach does not reference Azure-native protection with immutability
findings.Description: The requirement lists snapshot, copy, archive, and database dump strategies but does not reference Azure Backup vaulted backups with immutability or cross-region restore as recommended for ransomware protection and standardized recovery.

findings.Issue: Disaster recovery testing cadence not aligned with policy guidance
findings.Description: The section proposes quarterly backup/restore testing alerts but does not mention formal documented disaster recovery exercises or failover testing. Policy guidance recommends at least annual DR plan testing with documented outcomes.

findings.Issue: Centralized monitoring and logging requirements not defined
findings.Description: Policies require all workloads to emit telemetry to a central Log Analytics workspace with diagnostic settings enabled and integration with Microsoft Sentinel for security analytics. The section does not define monitoring, logging, or telemetry requirements.

findings.Issue: Mandatory tagging and cost governance not addressed
findings.Description: The policy requires mandatory tags (costCenter, owner, environment, workload, dataClassification, slaTier) enforced via Azure Policy along with budgets and cost alerts. The section mentions cost reduction goals but does not define the required tagging and governance model.

chunks.chunk_preview: h> High 12 RPO (Recovery Point Objective): 1h for Git, 1d for JIRA and Application - Zero data loss within RPO requirements

findings.Issue: Data classification not defined
findings.Description: The section defines encryption policies but does not specify a data classification model or a data inventory mapping datasets to sensitivity tiers (public, internal, confidential, restricted) that drives protection controls.

findings.Issue: Customer-managed keys not specified for encryption at rest
findings.Description: The section states that data at rest will rely on Azure Storage's out-of-the-box encryption features, which implies platform-managed keys. Policy requires customer-managed keys stored in Azure Key Vault Premium with HSM-backed keys for confidential and restricted data tiers.

findings.Issue: TLS version not explicitly enforced
findings.Description: The section specifies HTTPS for data in transit but does not explicitly require TLS 1.2 or higher or the disabling of TLS 1.0/1.1 as required by the policy.

findings.Issue: Key rotation policy not defined
findings.Description: The section does not mention automated key rotation or versioning for encryption keys. Policy requires key rotation on a maximum 12-month cadence with versioning preserved.

chunks.chunk_preview: ### 1.4 Review and verification of customer requirements and associated changes  · Ingram Micro Cloud Architect make sure to have detailed analysis of customer

findings.Issue: Changes not defined through Infrastructure as Code
findings.Description: The section states that the migration team will make associated changes after customer review but does not indicate that Azure infrastructure changes are implemented using Infrastructure as Code. Policy requires all Azure infrastructure to be defined as code (Bicep preferred, Terraform acceptable) rather than manual modification.

findings.Issue: No Git-based source control or pull request governance for changes
findings.Description: The change process described relies on internal review and customer verification but does not reference Git as the source of truth, branch protection, required reviewers, signed commits, or PR-based validation (linting, security scanning, and plan/what-if review) as required by the policy.

findings.Issue: CI/CD pipeline and environment promotion process not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not describe the required CI/CD pipeline process where deployments are promoted through environments (dev, test, stage, prod) with approval gates, automated smoke tests, and controlled production deployment windows.

chunks.chunk_preview: ### 1.5 Scope and business benefits  The following deliverables and business benefit from the project "SQL Database migration".   <table> <caption>Table 2 Scope

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO not defined for the workload
findings.Description: The section describes migration scope and benefits but does not declare Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO). Policy requires every production workload to define RPO and RTO during design review to drive redundancy, backup frequency, and disaster recovery patterns.

findings.Issue: Database security and access model not defined
findings.Description: The scope does not state that database authentication will use Microsoft Entra ID only, nor that SQL logins and built-in admin accounts will be disabled after bootstrap. Policies require Entra ID authentication and managed identities for service-to-database access.

findings.Issue: Private networking for database services not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not mention deploying Azure SQL Managed Instance or other data services with Private Endpoints and no public access. Policy requires relational workloads to be deployed inside delegated subnets with private connectivity and public network access disabled.

findings.Issue: Encryption and database security controls not specified
findings.Description: There is no reference to Transparent Data Encryption using customer-managed keys, database auditing, or Microsoft Defender for SQL with alerts to the SOC. These protections are required for confidential or restricted data according to policy.

findings.Issue: Centralized logging and telemetry not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not indicate that diagnostic settings, application telemetry, and platform logs will be streamed to a central Log Analytics workspace with Application Insights. Policy requires all workloads to emit telemetry and enable diagnostic settings on every resource.

findings.Issue: Use of Key Vault for secrets not mentioned
findings.Description: The scope does not state that application settings and database connection strings will use Azure Key Vault references and that secrets will not be stored directly in configuration. Policy requires universal use of Key Vault for secret storage.

chunks.chunk_preview: ### 1.6 Deployment Window Planning  The cutover date & time is confirmed and documented in the Solution Design Document with considering for minimal business di

findings.Issue: Deployment annotations to Application Insights not addressed
findings.Description: The section defines a deployment window but does not mention emitting deployment annotations to Application Insights during production deployments so observability dashboards can correlate behavioral changes with releases.

chunks.chunk_preview: ### 1.7 Cost analysis  General purpose tier is available with:  · 8 cores: $736.29/month  · 16 cores: $1,472.58/month  · 32 cores: $2,208.87/month  A deployment

findings.Issue: Mandatory cost management tags not defined
findings.Description: The section provides pricing estimates but does not specify the mandatory tagging scheme (costCenter, owner, environment, workload, dataClassification, slaTier) required on subscriptions, resource groups, and resources, nor the enforcement via Azure Policy.

findings.Issue: Budgets and cost alerting not addressed
findings.Description: The section lists estimated service costs but does not define Azure budgets or alerts at 50%, 80%, and 100% of forecast, nor the required action group notifications to workload owners and FinOps channels.

findings.Issue: Cost ownership and monitoring process not defined
findings.Description: The section does not establish cost ownership, ongoing cost monitoring practices, or reference reviews of Azure Advisor recommendations, reservations/savings plans evaluation, or anomaly detection as required by policy.

findings.Issue: Non-standard naming convention retained
findings.Description: The document states that an existing naming convention will be retained rather than following the prescribed deterministic pattern `<resourceType>-<workload>-<environment>-<region>-<instance>` with approved abbreviations.

findings.Issue: Global resource naming uniqueness strategy not documented
findings.Description: The section does not specify the required approach for globally unique resources (e.g., Storage accounts, Key Vaults, App Services, Container Registries) that must append a short hash suffix derived from the subscription ID to prevent naming collisions.

findings.Issue: Privileged access management not defined
findings.Description: The section does not describe how privileged roles will be granted or managed (e.g., just-in-time activation, approval workflows, or quarterly access reviews) as required by the identity governance guidance.

findings.Issue: Conditional Access and MFA requirements not addressed
findings.Description: There is no mention of Conditional Access policies or phishing-resistant MFA for administrators or users accessing production data planes, which are required controls.

findings.Issue: Managed identity strategy not documented
findings.Description: The section does not indicate whether system-assigned or user-assigned managed identities will be used for workload authentication to Azure services, which is the preferred identity model.

findings.Issue: Least-privilege role assignment approach not defined
findings.Description: The roles and responsibilities table does not describe how RBAC assignments will follow the least-privilege principle, including scoping to the smallest resource boundary and preference for built-in roles.

findings.Issue: Break-glass account governance not described
findings.Description: The section does not mention the required governance for emergency break-glass accounts, including Conditional Access exclusions, monitoring, and secure offline storage.

chunks.chunk_preview: # 2. Current architecture  ABC Ltd. has application and database servers deployed in multiple location in Australia. In Australia east location Townville has th

findings.Issue: Region names do not align with Azure regions
findings.Description: The section references 'Australia east location Townville' and 'Australia southeast location Adelaide'. These are not valid Azure region names. Policies require region selection aligned with Azure regions and region-pair strategy. The architecture description should reference official Azure regions (e.g., Australia East, Australia Southeast) and confirm their use as a region pair for resiliency.

findings.Issue: Region pair usage not explicitly stated
findings.Description: Policies state that deployments should use Azure region pairs for cross-region replication. Although Australia East and Australia Southeast are a valid pair, the section does not explicitly state that the secondary site participates in a region-pair replication or disaster recovery configuration.

findings.Issue: Hub-and-spoke network architecture not described
findings.Description: The policy requires Azure network design to start from a hub-and-spoke topology with shared services in a regional hub virtual network. The current architecture section does not mention hub-and-spoke networking, shared services, or traffic inspection via a hub.

findings.Issue: Target Azure database platform not specified
findings.Description: The section only shows an on‑premises diagram and does not specify the intended Azure relational database platform (e.g., Azure SQL Managed Instance or Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server) as preferred for relational workloads.

findings.Issue: Private connectivity and public access controls not defined
findings.Description: The section does not state that the database will be deployed with Private Endpoints, inside a delegated subnet, and with public network access disabled as required for PaaS data services.

findings.Issue: Database authentication model not defined
findings.Description: There is no indication that database authentication will use Microsoft Entra ID only with SQL logins and built-in admin accounts disabled after bootstrap and managed identities used for service-to-database calls.

findings.Issue: Encryption and security monitoring controls not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not describe the use of Transparent Data Encryption with customer-managed keys, database auditing, or Microsoft Defender for SQL with alerts to the SOC for confidential or restricted data.

findings.Issue: Schema migration governance not defined
findings.Description: There is no mention that schema migrations must be executed through version-controlled pipelines with approval gates and that ad-hoc DDL against production is prohibited.

findings.Issue: Database secrets management not defined
findings.Description: The section does not specify that database connection strings must be stored in Azure Key Vault and consumed via Key Vault references rather than stored directly in application settings.

findings.Issue: Backup and recovery objectives not declared
findings.Description: The section does not define database RPO/RTO targets or describe how backup retention and recovery capabilities will align with those objectives.

findings.Issue: No evaluation of managed compute alternatives before using VMs
findings.Description: The section lists application servers but does not document whether the workload was evaluated against managed compute platforms (App Service, Container Apps, Functions, AKS) before approving IaaS virtual machines, as required by policy.

findings.Issue: Golden image and CIS hardening not specified for VMs
findings.Description: The section does not indicate that virtual machines will be deployed from a curated golden image produced by Azure Image Builder or Azure Compute Gallery with CIS Level 1 or higher hardening baked in.

findings.Issue: Trusted Launch requirement not addressed
findings.Description: The design does not state that Trusted Launch will be enabled for Generation 2 virtual machines to provide Secure Boot, vTPM, and boot integrity monitoring.

findings.Issue: Just-in-Time access and Bastion management not defined
findings.Description: The section does not specify the use of Just-in-Time VM access via Microsoft Defender for Cloud or that Azure Bastion will be the only supported management path with no persistent inbound RDP or SSH exposure.

findings.Issue: VM patching and endpoint protection controls not defined
findings.Description: The section does not describe guest patching via Azure Update Manager within the required timelines or deployment of Microsoft Defender for Servers Plan 2 for endpoint protection and SOC alert forwarding.

chunks.chunk_preview: ### 2.1.2 Database Servers  <!-- PageBreak -->  <!-- PageNumber="Page | 10" -->   <table> <caption>Table 7 Existing on-prem database servers' inventory</caption

findings.Issue: Data classification inventory not documented
findings.Description: The section lists database servers but does not provide a data inventory mapping datasets to sensitivity tiers (public, internal, confidential, restricted) as required to drive protection controls.

findings.Issue: Encryption at rest configuration not specified
findings.Description: The inventory does not indicate whether encryption at rest is enabled for the databases or disks, nor whether customer-managed keys are used for sensitive tiers as required by policy.

findings.Issue: Encryption in transit requirements not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not state whether database connections enforce TLS 1.2 or higher with modern cipher suites, which is required for data in transit.

findings.Issue: Sensitive data field-level protection not defined
findings.Description: There is no indication that sensitive database fields are protected using mechanisms such as Always Encrypted, client-side encryption, or column-level encryption to mitigate insider access risks.

findings.Issue: Secrets management approach not defined
findings.Description: The section does not specify how database connection strings, credentials, or certificates are stored. Policy requires secrets to be stored exclusively in Azure Key Vault and referenced rather than embedded.

findings.Issue: Private network access pattern for databases not described
findings.Description: The design does not state whether databases will be deployed with Private Endpoints and with public access disabled, which is the preferred pattern for relational database services.

findings.Issue: Database authentication model not specified
findings.Description: The section does not indicate whether Microsoft Entra ID authentication will be used or whether SQL logins and built-in admin accounts will be disabled after bootstrap as required by the policy.

findings.Issue: Transparent Data Encryption with CMK not addressed
findings.Description: There is no mention of Transparent Data Encryption using customer-managed keys for confidential or restricted data tiers as required by database platform policy.

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO not defined
findings.Description: The section does not state that each production workload must declare Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO) during design review to drive backup frequency, redundancy, and disaster recovery patterns.

findings.Issue: Disaster recovery testing process not defined
findings.Description: The operational process does not mention the requirement to test disaster recovery plans at least annually through tabletop or live failover exercises and feed results back into architecture and SLO reviews.

findings.Issue: Centralized telemetry and Application Insights not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not specify that workloads must emit telemetry to a central Log Analytics workspace with workspace-based Application Insights for application telemetry.

findings.Issue: Diagnostic settings requirement not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not mention enabling diagnostic settings on all resources to forward activity logs, resource logs, and metrics to Log Analytics, which is required and treated as an audit control.

findings.Issue: Operational alerting and on-call integration not defined
findings.Description: The operational process does not define alerting practices such as burn-rate based alerts, Azure Monitor action groups, and routing alerts to on-call rotations (PagerDuty, ServiceNow, or Teams).

findings.Issue: Distributed tracing and correlation standards not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not mention distributed tracing using OpenTelemetry and the requirement for correlation IDs to propagate across services for end-to-end observability.

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO declaration not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not mention that each production workload must declare Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO) during design review, which drive redundancy, backup frequency, and disaster recovery patterns.

findings.Issue: Observability and diagnostic settings not assessed
findings.Description: The assessment description does not include validation that workloads emit telemetry to a central Log Analytics workspace, enable diagnostic settings on all resources, and use workspace-based Application Insights as required by policy.

findings.Issue: Cost ownership and mandatory tagging not included in assessment
findings.Description: The assessment section does not state that subscriptions, resource groups, and resources must include the mandatory tag set (costCenter, owner, environment, workload, dataClassification, slaTier) or that cost ownership is established at design time.

findings.Issue: Infrastructure as Code requirement not considered in assessment
findings.Description: The section does not mention validating that Azure infrastructure is defined as code (Bicep or approved Terraform usage), stored in Git with branch protection, and deployed through controlled CI/CD pipelines.

findings.Issue: Database security and private access evaluation not included
findings.Description: The assessment description does not mention verifying that relational databases are deployed with Private Endpoints, no public access, Microsoft Entra ID authentication, and other required database security controls.

# 5 Proposed architecture  <!-- PageBreak -->  <!-- PageNumber="Page | 11" -->  ABC Ltd.'s on-premise SQL database will be moved to Azure CSP by deploying resou

findings.Issue: Hub-and-spoke network topology not defined
findings.Description: The architecture diagram and description do not indicate a hub-and-spoke network topology with a central hub providing shared services (e.g., Azure Firewall, VPN/ExpressRoute gateways, DNS resolvers, Bastion). The policy states Azure network design starts from a hub-and-spoke topology with shared services in the hub.

findings.Issue: Centralized outbound internet egress via Azure Firewall not described
findings.Description: The section does not specify that outbound internet traffic from workload networks flows through Azure Firewall with FQDN-based rules, or that direct workload-to-internet routes are blocked as required by policy.

findings.Issue: WAF configuration details not specified
findings.Description: Although Application Gateway with WAF is shown, the section does not specify that WAF runs in prevention mode with the latest OWASP and Microsoft-managed rule sets as recommended by policy.

findings.Issue: Private Endpoints and public access restrictions for PaaS services not defined
findings.Description: The architecture references SQL Managed Instance but does not state that PaaS data services use Private Endpoints with public network access explicitly disabled, which is the default requirement in the policy.

findings.Issue: Private DNS zone integration not described
findings.Description: The section does not mention hub-managed Private DNS zones linked to all spokes for consistent name resolution of Private Endpoint–enabled services.

findings.Issue: DDoS Network Protection not specified for internet-facing virtual networks
findings.Description: The architecture includes internet ingress but does not indicate that DDoS Network Protection is enabled on the virtual network hosting the internet-facing endpoint as required by policy.

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO not defined for the workload
findings.Description: The section describes cross-region deployment and SQL failover but does not declare explicit Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO), which must be defined during design review to drive backup and disaster recovery decisions.

chunks.chunk_preview: ## The following sums up high level proposed Azure architecture:  · Existing VMs will be migrated into Azure VMs using lift-and-shift methods.  · Azure default

findings.Issue: Golden image and VM hardening not specified
findings.Description: The section states VMs will be migrated via lift‑and‑shift but does not specify that VMs must run from a curated golden image produced by Azure Image Builder or Azure Compute Gallery with CIS Level 1 or higher hardening.

findings.Issue: Trusted Launch configuration not addressed
findings.Description: The design does not state that Generation 2 VMs will use Trusted Launch with Secure Boot, vTPM, and boot integrity monitoring as required.

findings.Issue: Just-in-Time access and Bastion management path not defined
findings.Description: The architecture does not mention mandatory Just-in-Time VM access through Microsoft Defender for Cloud or the requirement that Azure Bastion be the only supported management path with no persistent inbound RDP/SSH ports.

findings.Issue: Endpoint protection and patching policy not described
findings.Description: There is no mention of Microsoft Defender for Servers Plan 2 deployment or Azure Update Manager patching timelines (critical/security patches within 14 days, others within 30).

findings.Issue: Subnet design does not follow tiered segmentation guidance
findings.Description: The design places application, utility, and reporting instances in the same subnet. Policies require purpose-built subnets for tiers (web, application, data, management) with segmentation enforced by NSGs.

findings.Issue: Hub-and-spoke network architecture not specified
findings.Description: The architecture does not indicate the required hub-and-spoke topology with shared services such as Azure Firewall, DNS resolvers, VPN/ExpressRoute gateways, and Bastion hosted in a central hub network.

findings.Issue: Direct VNet peering between workload networks
findings.Description: The document indicates that the Managed Instance VNet will be peered with another VNet/region. Policies state workload spokes should peer only with the hub and not directly with each other to ensure traffic inspection.

findings.Issue: Outbound internet traffic control not defined
findings.Description: The architecture does not state that outbound internet egress must flow through Azure Firewall with FQDN-based rules and that direct workload-to-internet routes should be blocked.

findings.Issue: Private Endpoint requirement for PaaS services not addressed
findings.Description: The design uses Azure SQL Managed Instance but does not specify Private Endpoint usage or that public network access must be disabled for PaaS data services.

findings.Issue: DDoS Network Protection not mentioned
findings.Description: The design includes an internet-facing Application Gateway but does not state that DDoS Network Protection will be enabled on the virtual network hosting internet-facing endpoints.

findings.Issue: Database security controls not specified
findings.Description: For Azure SQL Managed Instance, the section does not mention requirements such as Microsoft Entra ID–only authentication, Defender for SQL, auditing, or encryption controls aligned with policy guidance.

chunks.chunk_preview: ### 5.1 Solution Components  The Azure solution for this project has the following components and will be used within the solution.   <table> <tr> <th>SR#</th>

findings.Issue: Public Internet Exposure via Public IP
findings.Description: The design states that a Public IP address is required for the application to receive Internet traffic. Policy requires that public ingress be concentrated through Azure Front Door or Application Gateway with WAF, rather than exposing workloads directly via Public IPs.

findings.Issue: Jumpbox Exposed via Public RDP
findings.Description: The architecture proposes a jumpbox VM allowing RDP from a safelisted set of public IP addresses. Policy requires hub-based secure access patterns with bastion hosts and controlled network architecture; exposing RDP directly from the internet conflicts with the secure ingress model.

findings.Issue: Hub-and-Spoke Network Topology Not Defined
findings.Description: The section describes a single VNet with subnets but does not reference the required hub-and-spoke architecture with a regional hub providing shared services such as Azure Firewall, VPN/ExpressRoute gateways, DNS resolvers, and bastion hosts.

findings.Issue: NSG Rules Based on IP Safelists
findings.Description: The jumpbox NSG allows RDP from safelisted public IP ranges. Policy guidance prefers Network Security Groups with default-deny rules and explicit allow rules keyed to Application Security Groups rather than IP-based controls.

findings.Issue: Private Endpoints for PaaS Data Services
findings.Description: The architecture lists SQL Managed Instance but does not state that access will occur via Private Endpoints with public network access disabled, which is the required pattern for PaaS data services.

findings.Issue: Centralized Log Analytics Workspace
findings.Description: Azure Monitor is mentioned, but the section does not specify that telemetry and diagnostic settings from all resources must stream to a central Log Analytics workspace in the same geography.

findings.Issue: Mandatory Diagnostic Settings on All Resources
findings.Description: The section describes monitoring generally but does not state that platform diagnostic settings must be enabled on every resource and forwarded via Azure Policy to the central logging workspace.

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO Definition
findings.Description: The architecture section does not declare Recovery Point Objective (RPO) or Recovery Time Objective (RTO), which must be defined during design review and drive the DR and backup strategy.

findings.Issue: Backup and Disaster Recovery Strategy
findings.Description: No backup configuration or disaster recovery pattern is defined for VMs or the SQL Managed Instance. Policies require backups (e.g., Azure Backup for VMs and native protections like SQL PITR) aligned to the declared RPO/RTO.

chunks.chunk_preview: ### 5.2 High Availability (HA) architecture  The auto-failover group is configured on the primary instance and will connect to the secondary instance in a diffe

findings.Issue: Single-region Application Gateway creates availability gap
findings.Description: The section states that Application Gateway is deployed only in Australia East and the application is therefore not highly available. Reliability guidance describes DR patterns such as active-active or active-passive across paired regions. A single-region ingress component introduces a single point of failure and does not align with the recommended cross-region resiliency patterns.

findings.Issue: Manual disaster recovery process
findings.Description: The architecture relies on a manual DR process. The reliability guidance recommends defined DR patterns (for example active-active across paired regions or active-passive failover). Manual recovery introduces longer and unpredictable recovery times compared with structured failover architectures.

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO not defined
findings.Description: The section does not specify Recovery Point Objective (RPO) or Recovery Time Objective (RTO). Policy requires every production workload to declare RPO and RTO during design review so that redundancy, backup frequency, and disaster recovery architecture can be aligned with these targets.

findings.Issue: DR testing strategy not described
findings.Description: The section does not describe testing of the disaster recovery plan. Policy requires every workload to test its DR plan at least annually through tabletop or live failover exercises with documented results.

chunks.chunk_preview: ### 5.3 Failover Scenarios   <figure> <figcaption>Figure 2 Failover architecture</figcaption>  Internet Traffic  Traffic  Application Gateway  Application Gatew

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO not defined
findings.Description: The failover section does not state the Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO) that drive the disaster recovery design. Policy requires every production workload to declare RPO and RTO during design review so redundancy, backup frequency, and DR patterns can be validated.

findings.Issue: Disaster recovery testing not addressed
findings.Description: The section describes regional failover architecture but does not mention annual DR testing (tabletop or live failover). Policy requires every workload to test its DR plan at least annually and feed the results back into architecture and SLO reviews.

findings.Issue: Backup configuration and vault strategy not specified
findings.Description: The diagram references a Recovery Services Vault but the section does not describe backup configuration such as vaulted backups, immutability, ransomware protection, or cross‑region restore for tier workloads. Policies require Azure Backup with these protections and regional vault deployment.

findings.Issue: Separation of backup credentials and production keys not documented
findings.Description: The architecture section does not specify that backup credentials and recovery keys are stored in a separate Key Vault from production keys. Policy requires separation to reduce blast radius during compromise.

findings.Issue: Private endpoint and public access posture for SQL MI not specified
findings.Description: The failover design shows SQL Managed Instance primary and secondary but does not state that the instances are deployed with Private Endpoints and no public access as required for relational workloads.

chunks.chunk_preview: ### 5.4 Disaster recovery architecture  <!-- PageBreak -->  <!-- PageNumber="Page | 14" -->  With disaster recovery, Azure VMs continuously replicate from to a

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO not defined
findings.Description: The disaster recovery section describes VM replication and failover using Azure Site Recovery but does not declare the Recovery Point Objective (RPO) or Recovery Time Objective (RTO) for the workload. Policy requires these values to be defined during design review and to drive DR architecture choices.

findings.Issue: Backup strategy not documented
findings.Description: The section only describes replication via Azure Site Recovery and does not specify backup protections such as Azure Backup or platform-native backup configurations aligned to the declared RPO. Policy requires workloads to define backup protections (e.g., vaulted backups, immutability, or platform-native backup features) in addition to DR patterns.

findings.Issue: Recovery Services or Backup Vault configuration not described
findings.Description: There is no mention of Recovery Services Vaults or Backup Vaults per region or their diagnostic settings being forwarded to the central monitoring workspace, which is required by policy for backup and recovery management.

findings.Issue: Disaster recovery testing process not defined
findings.Description: The architecture does not describe how the DR plan will be tested. Policy requires each workload to test its DR plan at least annually through a documented tabletop or live failover exercise with results feeding back into architecture review.

chunks.chunk_preview: # 5 Architectural review  · Ingram Micro Cloud Architect make sure to setup meeting with customer requirements and share proposed architecture for review.  · On

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO not defined during architecture review
findings.Description: The architectural review section does not state that Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO) must be declared as part of the design review. The policy requires every production workload to define RPO and RTO during design review to guide redundancy, backup frequency, and disaster recovery patterns.

findings.Issue: Disaster recovery and backup strategy not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not include validation of backup configuration, DR patterns, or recovery testing as part of the architecture review. Policy requires alignment of backups and DR architecture (e.g., Azure Backup, native PaaS protection, region-pair DR patterns) with the declared RPO/RTO and regular DR testing.

findings.Issue: Cost management and mandatory tagging not validated
findings.Description: The architectural review description does not include verification of mandatory tagging (costCenter, owner, environment, workload, dataClassification, slaTier) or cost governance such as budgets and FinOps alerts, which are required by policy.

findings.Issue: Infrastructure-as-Code and CI/CD governance not included in review
findings.Description: The architecture review process does not mention validation that infrastructure is deployed through Infrastructure-as-Code (Bicep/Terraform), stored in Git with branch protection, and deployed through secured CI/CD pipelines using workload identity federation as required by policy.

findings.Issue: Azure Policy and compliance governance checks not included
findings.Description: The section does not state that architectural review validates Azure Policy assignments, regulatory compliance alignment, logging, audit retention, and formal policy exemption handling as required by governance policy.

chunks.chunk_preview: # 6 Migration Strategy   <table> <caption>Table 10 Refactor, Rehost and Rearchitect</caption> <tr> <th>Server Name</th> <th>Scope</th> <th>Tool</th> <th></th> <

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO not defined
findings.Description: The migration strategy does not declare Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO) for the production databases being migrated. Policy requires every production workload to define RPO and RTO during design review to drive backup, redundancy, and disaster recovery patterns.

findings.Issue: Target Azure database platform not specified
findings.Description: The section lists SQL servers to be rearchitected using DMA and DMS but does not specify the target Azure database service. Policy states relational workloads should prefer Azure SQL Managed Instance or Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server with private networking and no public access.

findings.Issue: Backup and disaster recovery approach for migrated databases not described
findings.Description: The migration plan does not describe how backups, point-in-time recovery, or cross-region recovery will be configured after migration. Policies require backup and recovery configurations aligned to the declared RPO/RTO and use of platform-native protections.

findings.Issue: Controlled schema migration process not addressed
findings.Description: The section references migration tools (DMA and DMS) but does not specify that schema migrations will be executed through version-controlled pipelines with approval gates. Policy requires that no ad-hoc DDL changes occur directly in production.

chunks.chunk_preview: # 7 Configuration Parameters  <!-- PageBreak -->  <!-- PageNumber="Page | 15" -->  During the build phase, the following parameters will be enabled throughout a

findings.Issue: Secret management via Key Vault not defined
findings.Description: The section does not specify how secrets or configuration values are managed. Policy requires that application settings store no secrets and that Key Vault references are used universally.

findings.Issue: HTTPS-only and TLS configuration not specified
findings.Description: The section does not state that HTTPS-only access and a minimum TLS 1.2 configuration will be enforced across services as required by policy.

findings.Issue: Diagnostic settings to Log Analytics not defined
findings.Description: The section does not specify enabling diagnostic settings for resources to stream logs, metrics, and activity data to Log Analytics. Policy states diagnostic settings must be enabled on every resource.

findings.Issue: Application telemetry and centralized monitoring not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not mention emitting telemetry to a central Log Analytics workspace or using workspace-based Application Insights for application telemetry.

findings.Issue: Health checks and auto-heal configuration not specified
findings.Description: The configuration parameters section does not define health-check endpoints or auto-heal rules required for automatically recycling unhealthy instances.

chunks.chunk_preview: ## 7.1 Network Configuration  In a migration process, set up networking is one of the critical design steps. Below mentioned point s are considered in a typical

findings.Issue: Hub-and-spoke topology not defined
findings.Description: The section indicates an existing VNet will be used and explicitly states that VNet peering and Azure Virtual WAN are not configured. Policy guidance requires Azure network design to start with a hub-and-spoke topology where spokes peer to a central hub that hosts shared services such as firewall, gateways, DNS, and bastion.

findings.Issue: Application Security Groups not used for NSG rules
findings.Description: The design states that an existing NSG will be used and that Application Security Groups are not configured. The policy specifies that NSG rules should use explicit allow lists keyed to Application Security Groups rather than IP ranges.

findings.Issue: Centralized ingress via Front Door or Application Gateway with WAF not specified
findings.Description: The section does not describe how public ingress will be handled. Policy requires public ingress to be concentrated through Azure Front Door or Application Gateway with Web Application Firewall in prevention mode using Microsoft-managed and OWASP rules.

findings.Issue: Outbound internet routing through Azure Firewall not defined
findings.Description: There is no mention of routing outbound internet traffic through Azure Firewall. Policy requires outbound egress to pass through Azure Firewall with FQDN-based rules and direct workload-to-internet routes to be blocked.

findings.Issue: Private Endpoints for PaaS services not addressed
findings.Description: The network configuration section does not mention the use of Private Endpoints or disabling public network access for PaaS data services. Policy states Private Endpoints must be the default for services such as Storage, Key Vault, SQL, Cosmos DB, Service Bus, and Event Hubs.

findings.Issue: Private DNS zone strategy not defined
findings.Description: The design does not describe Private DNS zones or their linkage to virtual networks. Policy requires hub-managed Private DNS zones linked to all spokes to ensure consistent name resolution for Private Endpoints.

findings.Issue: DDoS Network Protection not specified
findings.Description: The network configuration does not state whether DDoS Network Protection is enabled on the virtual network. Policy requires DDoS Network Protection to be enabled on every VNet hosting an internet-facing endpoint.

findings.Issue: Subnet tier segmentation not defined
findings.Description: The section only mentions adding a gateway subnet and does not define purpose-built subnets for workload tiers (such as web, application, data, and management). Policy requires each spoke to be subdivided into tier-based subnets with NSG controls.

chunks.chunk_preview: # 8 Risk Identification and Mitigation  The Risk Identification and Migration section of the Solution Design Document details the process and company specific r

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO not defined
findings.Description: The section does not declare Recovery Point Objective (RPO) or Recovery Time Objective (RTO) values for the workload. Policies require every production workload to define RPO and RTO during design review to drive backup frequency, redundancy, and disaster recovery design.

findings.Issue: Observability and diagnostic configuration risks not addressed
findings.Description: The risk section does not address risks or mitigation related to mandatory observability requirements such as enabling diagnostic settings on all resources, sending telemetry to a central Log Analytics workspace, or configuring Application Insights and alerting. These controls are required and their absence represents an unaddressed operational risk.

findings.Issue: Secrets management controls not covered
findings.Description: The section does not identify or mitigate risks related to secret management. Policies require that application settings store no secrets and that Key Vault references be used universally. The risk process should explicitly account for this control.

findings.Issue: Infrastructure as Code and CI/CD governance risks not covered
findings.Description: The risk management section does not address risks associated with infrastructure deployment practices. Policies require infrastructure to be defined as code (Bicep/Terraform), stored in Git with branch protection, and deployed through controlled CI/CD pipelines using workload-identity federation.

findings.Issue: Database security and network isolation risks not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not identify risks or mitigations related to database platform requirements such as Private Endpoint access, no public exposure, Microsoft Entra ID authentication, and managed identity usage for service-to-database access.

chunks.chunk_preview: # 9 Optimizing Workloads  <!-- PageBreak -->  <!-- PageNumber="Page | 16" -->  After Ingram Micro moves resources to Azure, they need to streamline them to impr

findings.Issue: Centralized telemetry and monitoring not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not mention emitting telemetry to a central Log Analytics workspace, workspace-based Application Insights, or enabling diagnostic settings for resources. The policy requires all workloads to send telemetry and platform diagnostics to a central workspace for monitoring and auditing.

findings.Issue: Alerting and SLO monitoring not defined
findings.Description: The section does not describe defining service-level objectives (SLOs), SLI dashboards, or alerting through Azure Monitor action groups to on-call systems. Policies require burn-rate–based alerting and operational runbook linkage.

findings.Issue: Mandatory tagging strategy not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not reference the required tagging model for subscriptions, resource groups, and resources (`costCenter`, `owner`, `environment`, `workload`, `dataClassification`, `slaTier`). Policy requires these tags to be enforced via Azure Policy.

findings.Issue: Budgets and cost alerting not defined
findings.Description: Although cost optimization is discussed, the section does not specify configuring budgets with alerts at 50%, 80%, and 100% of forecast with action groups notifying FinOps and workload owners as required by policy.

findings.Issue: Cost governance practices not included
findings.Description: The section does not mention reviewing Azure Advisor recommendations monthly, evaluating reservations or savings plans, or enabling cost anomaly detection and automated investigation tasks, which are required cost management practices.

findings.Issue: VM scheduling guidance not aligned with non-production policy
findings.Description: The section suggests scheduling VM snooze/unsnooze generally, but policy specifically requires non-production environments (dev/test) to automatically shut down outside business hours and ephemeral environments to be torn down via IaC pipelines. The guidance should explicitly align with this pattern.

chunks.chunk_preview: ## 9.1 Azure Cost Management  · To make the most of their cloud investment, Ingram Micro will take advantage of the free Azure Cost Management tool.  · Azure Co

findings.Issue: Cost ownership definition not specified
findings.Description: The section does not state that cost ownership is established at design time for the workload, subscription, or resource groups as required by the policy.

findings.Issue: Mandatory tagging strategy not defined
findings.Description: The section does not specify the required tag set (costCenter, owner, environment, workload, dataClassification, slaTier) or the use of Azure Policy to deny resource creation when mandatory tags are missing.

findings.Issue: Budgets and alert thresholds not defined
findings.Description: The section does not describe configuring budgets per subscription and per workload with alerts at 50%, 80%, and 100% of forecast or notifying the FinOps channel and workload owner via action groups.

findings.Issue: Cost anomaly detection not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not mention enabling cost anomaly detection and triggering automated investigation tasks when anomalies occur.

findings.Issue: Non-production cost controls not defined
findings.Description: The section does not address policy requirements for non-production environments such as scheduled auto-shutdown of dev/test compute outside business hours and teardown of ephemeral environments via IaC pipelines.

findings.Issue: FinOps reporting and exports not described
findings.Description: The section does not specify exporting Microsoft Cost Management data to a central FinOps workspace (e.g., Power BI) for chargeback and showback reporting.

findings.Issue: Reserved capacity and savings plans evaluation not mentioned
findings.Description: The section does not mention evaluating reservations and savings plans quarterly for steady-state workloads or using spot VMs for fault-tolerant batch workloads as recommended by the policy.

chunks.chunk_preview: ## 9.2 Total cost of ownership (TCO)   <table> <caption>Table 12 Cost Analysis</caption> <tr> <th>On-premises cost (per month/year)</th> <th>Azure cost</th> <th

findings.Issue: Cost ownership definition absent
findings.Description: The section provides a basic TCO comparison but does not establish cost ownership for the workload. Policy requires cost ownership to be defined at design time.

findings.Issue: Mandatory tagging strategy not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not mention the mandatory tag set (costCenter, owner, environment, workload, dataClassification, slaTier) or enforcement via Azure Policy for subscriptions, resource groups, and resources.

findings.Issue: Budget and alert configuration not defined
findings.Description: The design does not specify budgets per subscription and per workload with alerts at 50%, 80%, and 100% of forecast and action group notifications as required by policy.

findings.Issue: FinOps operational practices not included
findings.Description: The section does not address required cost governance practices such as quarterly evaluation of reservations and savings plans, use of spot VMs for fault-tolerant workloads, or monthly Azure Advisor cost optimization reviews.

findings.Issue: Non-production cost controls not documented
findings.Description: The design does not specify scheduling controls such as dev/test auto-shutdown outside business hours or teardown of ephemeral environments via IaC pipelines.

findings.Issue: Cost monitoring and reporting mechanisms absent
findings.Description: The section does not include cost anomaly detection, automated investigation triggers, or chargeback/showback reporting using Microsoft Cost Management exports to a central FinOps workspace.

chunks.chunk_preview: ## 10.1 Secure Migrated Workloads  Securing workloads from threats is the most critical task after migration. Below security measures can be taken to avoid inte

findings.Issue: Single Entra ID tenant and centralized identity model not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not state that all human and workload identities must originate from a single Microsoft Entra ID tenant or describe how identities for workloads will be issued and governed.

findings.Issue: Privileged Identity Management not defined
findings.Description: The section does not mention just-in-time privileged access using Privileged Identity Management with time-bound activation, approval workflows, and quarterly access reviews for administrative roles.

findings.Issue: Conditional Access and phishing‑resistant MFA requirements not defined
findings.Description: Although MFA is listed, the section does not specify enforcement through Conditional Access or require phishing‑resistant MFA for administrators and users accessing production data planes, nor does it mention blocking legacy authentication.

findings.Issue: Managed identity usage for workloads not specified
findings.Description: The section does not state that Azure managed identities should be the preferred mechanism for workload authentication or restrict the use of service principals with client secrets.

findings.Issue: Least privilege RBAC implementation not detailed
findings.Description: The section recommends reviewing IAM access but does not specify that role assignments should follow least privilege principles, use built‑in roles where possible, and scope access to the smallest resource boundary.

findings.Issue: Hub-and-spoke network segmentation not addressed
findings.Description: The section does not describe the required hub-and-spoke network architecture with centralized services such as Azure Firewall, VPN/ExpressRoute gateways, DNS resolvers, and Bastion, or the requirement to prevent spoke-to-spoke traffic.

findings.Issue: Private Endpoints for PaaS services not specified
findings.Description: The section does not require PaaS services such as Storage, Key Vault, SQL, Cosmos DB, Service Bus, or Event Hubs to be accessed via Private Endpoints with public network access disabled.

findings.Issue: Controlled ingress and egress architecture not defined
findings.Description: The section mentions WAF but does not require that public ingress be centralized through Azure Front Door or Application Gateway with WAF in prevention mode, nor that outbound internet access flow through Azure Firewall with direct workload-to-internet routes blocked.

findings.Issue: DDoS Network Protection requirement not mentioned
findings.Description: The section does not specify enabling DDoS Network Protection on virtual networks hosting internet-facing endpoints.

findings.Issue: Data classification and sensitivity tiers not defined
findings.Description: The section discusses encryption but does not define a data classification model mapping datasets to sensitivity tiers (public, internal, confidential, restricted) to determine encryption, retention, and access controls.

findings.Issue: Customer-managed key requirement not addressed for encryption
findings.Description: The section mentions Azure Disk Encryption and default storage encryption but does not require customer-managed keys stored in Azure Key Vault Premium with HSM-backed keys for confidential or restricted data tiers.

findings.Issue: Key rotation policy not defined
findings.Description: The section does not describe automated encryption key rotation with a maximum 12‑month cadence and version retention for historical data decryption.

findings.Issue: TLS version enforcement not specified
findings.Description: The section does not require TLS 1.2 or higher for encryption in transit or specify disabling TLS 1.0/1.1 where supported.

findings.Issue: Key Vault usage not fully defined
findings.Description: Azure Key Vault is mentioned for application secrets, but the section does not explicitly require that all secrets such as connection strings, API keys, and certificates be stored exclusively in Key Vault and referenced from configuration or templates.

chunks.chunk_preview: ## 10.2 Manage Migrated Workloads  After securing the resources, the following features will be turned on to ensure workloads are managed as standard to minimiz

findings.Issue: Diagnostic logging not aligned to central Log Analytics requirement
findings.Description: The section only states 'Enable diagnostic logs' without specifying that diagnostic settings must stream application logs, HTTP logs, resource logs, activity logs, and metrics to a central Log Analytics workspace via Azure Policy. The policy requires all resources to forward telemetry to a central workspace and treats absence of diagnostic settings as an audit failure.

findings.Issue: Centralized observability stack not defined
findings.Description: The section does not mention workspace-based Application Insights, central Log Analytics workspace usage, OpenTelemetry distributed tracing, SLI dashboards, or alert routing through Azure Monitor action groups as required for workload observability.

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO definitions for disaster recovery
findings.Description: The section mentions implementing Site Recovery and business continuity but does not define Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO). Policy requires every production workload to declare RPO and RTO during design review and use them to determine backup frequency and DR architecture.

findings.Issue: VM security baseline and image governance not addressed
findings.Description: VM management guidance only references availability groups and managed disks. The policy requires VMs to run from curated golden images produced by Azure Image Builder or Azure Compute Gallery with CIS Level 1 hardening and Trusted Launch enabled.

findings.Issue: Managed disk configuration lacks policy-recommended defaults
findings.Description: The section recommends using managed disks but does not specify the policy preference for Premium SSD v2 disks for production workloads or guidance around host-based caching and ephemeral OS disks for stateless workers.

chunks.chunk_preview: #### 11.1 Problem/Incident Management  In the Hypercare period the team is available 24/7.  <!-- PageFooter="P1 issues" --> <!-- PageFooter="Troubleshooting ste

findings.Issue: Public firewall access suggested for Azure SQL
findings.Description: The troubleshooting step recommends allowing the client IP through Azure SQL firewall rules. Policies require relational databases to be deployed with Private Endpoints and no public access. Allowing client IP access through the public firewall implies public network exposure, which contradicts the requirement for Private Endpoint–only connectivity.

findings.Issue: Troubleshooting approach bypasses private network design
findings.Description: The guidance focuses on modifying Azure SQL firewall rules to restore connectivity instead of validating Private Endpoint connectivity, private DNS resolution, or hub-and-spoke network inspection paths. Policy requires PaaS data services to be accessed through Private Endpoints with public network access disabled.

findings.Issue: No reference to centralized logging or telemetry for incident diagnosis
findings.Description: The incident management section does not mention using centralized telemetry such as Log Analytics, Application Insights, or platform diagnostic settings to investigate issues. Policy requires workloads to emit telemetry to a central Log Analytics workspace and have diagnostic settings enabled for operational troubleshooting.

chunks.chunk_preview: #### 11.2 Change Management  Below table has the change management topics that can be taken care if required   <table> <caption>Table 16 Change management</capt

findings.Issue: Ad-hoc changes allowed without formal review
findings.Description: The section allows ad-hoc and minor changes to be absorbed without going through a formal process if the effort is less than 2–3 hours. The AI Workloads policy requires that agent and tool definition changes be managed as version-controlled prompt contracts and that changes go through code review. Allowing ad-hoc changes without a defined review or version-control process conflicts with this requirement.

findings.Issue: No requirement for version-controlled prompt contracts and code review
findings.Description: The change management section does not mention that AI agent or tool definition changes must be maintained as version-controlled prompt contracts with declared intent, inputs, outputs, edge cases, and acceptance criteria, and that updates must go through code review as required by the AI Workloads policy.

chunks.chunk_preview: ### 11.3 Availability Measurement  High availability of the Azure services such that the SLA requirements are met   <table> <caption>Table 17 Availability measu

findings.Issue: Service-level objectives and indicators not defined
findings.Description: The section lists Azure service SLA percentages but does not define workload-level Service Level Objectives (SLOs) or Service Level Indicators (SLIs) for critical user journeys, nor how availability will be measured through dashboards or telemetry. The policy requires defining SLOs per critical user journey and tracking them through monitoring tools.

findings.Issue: Availability measurement relies on platform SLA instead of workload telemetry
findings.Description: Availability is represented only by Azure service SLA values (e.g., Azure SQL Database and App Service 99.99%). Policy guidance expects availability measurement to be derived from workload telemetry and monitoring (e.g., SLI dashboards, burn-rate alerts) rather than relying solely on Azure service SLAs.

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO not declared
findings.Description: The section does not declare Recovery Point Objective (RPO) or Recovery Time Objective (RTO) for the production workload. The policy states that every production workload must declare RPO and RTO during design review to drive redundancy, backup frequency, and disaster recovery patterns.

chunks.chunk_preview: # Appendix A: Comments and Response   <table> <caption>Table 18 Feedback - comment and response</caption> <tr> <th>SR#</th> <th>Reviewer Name</th> <th>Reviewer'
# Appendix B - Peer Review Process  The Solution Peer Review Process is detailed in this section.  <!-- PageBreak -->  <!-- PageNumber="Page | 20" -->   <figure

findings.Issue: RPO and RTO declaration not included in design review criteria
findings.Description: The peer review process lists fit-for-use, requirements coverage, business drivers, and best practices as review criteria, but it does not include validation that the workload declares Recovery Point Objective (RPO) and Recovery Time Objective (RTO) during design review. The policy states that every production workload must declare RPO and RTO during design review and that these values drive redundancy, backup frequency, and disaster recovery patterns.
